name: White Bean and Vegetable Cassoulet with Millet Crust
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces yellow crookneck squash, trimmed and cut into ½-inch pieces
4 ounces green beans, cut into 2-inch pieces
2 teaspoons harissa paste (or ½ teaspoon dried crushed red pepper)
1 can diced tomatoes in juice (28-ounce can)
1 can cannellini beans (white kidney beans), rinsed and drained (15-ounce can)
½ cup breadcrumbs made from French bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large pot over medium heat. Add the red bell peppers, onions, carrots, and garlic. Sauté until tender, about 15 minutes.
2. Add the squash and green beans to the pot and stir for 1 minute.
3. Stir in the cumin and harissa paste, then add the diced tomatoes with juice. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to medium and cook until the mixture thickens slightly, stirring occasionally, about 12 minutes.
5. Mix in the cannellini beans and ½ cup basil. Transfer the mixture to a 13x9x2-inch glass baking dish. (This can be made ahead of time, covered, and chilled.)
6. Heat 1 teaspoon olive oil in a medium nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add the millet and stir until lightly golden, about 5 minutes.
7. Stir in the water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium, cover, and cook until the millet is tender and the liquid is almost absorbed, about 20 minutes. Drain the millet.
8. Transfer the cooked millet to a bowl and cool. Mix in the breadcrumbs and remaining teaspoon oil.
9. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Sprinkle the millet mixture evenly over the vegetables in the baking dish.
10. Bake until the vegetables are heated through and the topping begins to crisp, about 35 minutes.
11. Sprinkle the remaining ½ cup basil around the edges and serve.
